title: Protocol Outcomes, Layers, Standards, and Evidence · Execution Trace: Normative Authority
summary: A team says it built HTTP from scratch because one request works, but it cannot separate application semantics, wire syntax, transport behavior, implementation policy, or user and operator evidence. Planned artifact: Add and verify the normative authority increment in protocol outcome, layer, authority, and evidence charter. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use current RFC requirements, errata, Go documentation, and implementation tests as distinct authorities and resolve conflicts explicitly.
Trace application policy, HTTP semantics, HTTP/1.1 syntax, TLS records, TCP byte streams, IP delivery, and local socket APIs without collapsing layers.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Predict Normative Authority · Theory Normative Authority
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory protocol team handles case 1084 in a municipal gateway receiving one-byte TCP fragments. Build deterministic Go evidence to separate model, wire, state, and authorized live-transfer evidence; preserve this operating constraint: one connection has exactly one read owner and one terminal close owner; then truncate one content octet and classify incomplete framing without connection reuse. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Normative Authority contract for the supplied data.

Evidence target: Predict Normative Authority · Theory Normative Authority.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for use current rfc requirements, errata, go documentation, and implementation tests as distinct authorities and resolve conflicts explicitly. For Predict Normative Authority · Theory Normative Authority, keep Normative Authority connected to executable Go behavior evidence.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Declare types, ownership, goroutine lifetime, failure contract, and observable result; trace value copies, aliases, blocking, and happens-before edges; run the smallest isolated program; then change one boundary input and retain a regression invariant. Apply that move specifically to Normative Authority while producing protocol outcome, layer, authority, and evidence charter.

steps.title: Build Normative Authority evidence · Theory Normative Authority
steps.instruction:
Append a compile-ready pure-Go function headed "// Evidence: httpproto-normative-authority-1la64b" that uses case 3603 to separate model, wire, state, and authorized live-transfer evidence. Return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not open sockets or listeners, negotiate TLS, contact networks or proxies, capture packets, read host state, or use credentials; verify those boundaries later with the named Go 1.26 protocol transfer gates.

Evidence target: Build Normative Authority evidence · Theory Normative Authority.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Normative Authority into observable performance evidence. For Build Normative Authority evidence · Theory Normative Authority, keep Normative Authority connected to executable Go behavior evidence.

steps.title: Defend the Normative Authority model · Theory Normative Authority
steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Normative Authority: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory protocol team handles case 1090 in a multi-tenant intake service near its connection and memory ceilings. Build deterministic Go evidence to separate model, wire, state, and authorized live-transfer evidence; preserve this operating constraint: one connection has exactly one read owner and one terminal close owner; then force a short write and retain the exact transmitted prefix plus remaining count. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Use current RFC requirements, errata, Go documentation, and implementation tests as distinct authorities and resolve conflicts explicitly.

Evidence target: Defend the Normative Authority model · Theory Normative Authority.
steps.why: Retrieving Normative Authority through the concrete protocol outcome, layer, authority, and evidence charter case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For Defend the Normative Authority model · Theory Normative Authority, keep Normative Authority connected to executable Go behavior evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Use current RFC requirements, errata, Go documentation, and implementation tests as distinct authorities and resolve conflicts explicitly.
